item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image has since been removed.

item.title: Image provenance
item.content:encoded:
Grisha Dubrovsky makes little felt animals that he sells on Etsy. He's got dozens of photos of them on his website. This is one of them.

https://feltanimals.com/pages/image/imagepage36.html

I see no indication that his photos are available under Creative Commons; however, he makes enough monster toys that it might be worth contacting him to ask permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a vaguely interesting scip that lacks a lot of the details I look for in articles. I'm not going to retread old ground already covered in previous posts but the lack of any experimentation really hurts this scip. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't need to do that, really, as pastarasta1 said, a lot of people have the same opinions, it's just that both comments were very similar so this was suspicious, but don't bother taking such care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh ok. I will take more care in the future to make sure my comments are unique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's impossible to delete your own comment, but don't worry, a little redundancy never hurt anyone.
You don't need to delete.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't read anyone's post like this, but I can delete mine

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is literally almost the exact same thing pig_Catapult said, not being rude, but I have a feeling you took it from them.

Then again, tons of other people are saying the same thing, I guess it's just shared opinions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First I was like: Aw, its a stuffed centipede, won't be that bad.

Next: I have a REALLY bad feeling…

At the end: …..fuck

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all Scorpion: they aren't parasites. they don't negatively affect the hosts health for there own gain. Second: as soulless has said earlier in the comments, they use a minor spatial anomaly to move, and that same anomaly makes them impossible to be removed.

(sorry if this seemed rude, i didn't mean it in that way)

Oh, and i have a question for soulless as well. does "loveus / love us" have any significance to the article, or is it just there because… our "mutual" acquaintance that tends to be a kind man… decided that he could have some "surcret" message?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They probably can and do remove the things, but parasites usually don't live long without a host. Protect works both ways, so they're keeping 5 of them alive in captivity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there any reason these things can't just be surgically removed from hosts? Why is it necessary that -A instances be kept like they are?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It didn't really impress me all that much, mostly for reasons already explained by others, until the very end. I've never really been a fan of parasites, and I've already seen the 'crawling in your skin' shtick.
However…
That last addendum made me shiver in my seat.
It also convinced me to +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The mainlist title: it lies

Anyways, this one established the fact that it's a pattern that centipedes in this wiki are horrible, horrible things, just as most fox-related articles involve some horrible form of sex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How would you go about surgically removing something that mobilizes around the body via minor space anomaly?

You'd better notice this impossibility in description or containment procedures block.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initial reaction: D'awww, adorable centipede is adorable.
After finishing article: … fuck.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, in hindsight, it was evident that this baby was Soulless' baby. Upvoted without a care. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I reeeaaallly want to upvote this. It's a horrid adorable little cute felt monster that takes away part of your humanity—or at least makes you regret everytime you're humanly nostalgic anyway.
But, why in the world wouldn't someone try to remove such a wretched creature? The article doesn't address this extremely pressing question.
So, I have to stay neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very creepy. The final addendum and expecially the part where it gets kinda stuck once or twice under the tight scalp skin really gave me the shivers. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It wants to make people feel better… and it goes about that by negatively reinforcing them every time they have a strong memory, good or bad…

I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
???

All I can really say is I'm sure he's happy that you noticed his presence. Maybe. He likes to travel, so it's hard to pin him down to be sure…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What are you trying to do with this "Kind man" which you have slapped into a bunch of your articles? >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I checked my arms after this, damn you.
Creepy factor to 11. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohgodthatlog…and the explaination of the squirming under the skin…and HEARING IT on the temples…

Have an upvote and my ability to sleep soundly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with the guy who mentioned testing by amputation of nonvital areas. Also, yeah, although it conveys ick, there's not enough information on what else is going on here. Breeding is unexplained, -A quarantine is unexplained… you need to add to this or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last note sells this article. It's so good. The rest of the article is at least pretty nice, with some bits I could do without, but the note is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy freaking crap what did I just read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They seem to roam pretty freely, watch for it to wander into a non-vital area and amputate a whole chunk around the bug.

Even if removal is impossible, for narrative reasons, it is an obvious avenue of experimentation. The Foundation would at least try, and document precisely how the procedure failed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh God oh God oh God…

This made me all itchy and grossed out…

That said, I "enjoyed" it very much. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because if you know what they do, you don't need more than a couple to keep around for general observation. Besides at that point it would practically be mercy killing.

(Also if you sincerely felt that this scp was purely about the "horror" of a felt centipede in you for the rest of your life I'm sorry I've failed to deliver my point.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Should the SCP-1353-A population exceed more than 5, the oldest and least healthy are to be culled"
"Hosts continue to function as normal human beings"

Why is the Foundation killing civilians who are moderately discomforted and pose no discernible threat ?

Something crawling under one's skin is indeed creepy, but there are actual real life medical conditions where a parasite lives inside a human host and can be perceived by that host; there is no fiction needed to convey that particular horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand that the centipede being inside a person is the interesting part of the SCP, and thus is what the article concentrates on, but I feel that the Foundation would report more details about their "life" outside of a person. Things like: how quickly do they grow? Do they ever need to eat anything (especially while growing)? Are the forcipules hard despite being made from felt, or are they capable of making incisions in spite of being soft? Do they freeze and pretend to be normal if exposed to light and/or observed by a human?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how, in reading this, I'm going, "Okay, so it crawls into you and then…"

And then you have a fucking felt centipede lodged under your skin for the rest of your life. Augh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like that both sides of the arrangement are given.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's got a lot of stuff going on with all the descriptions of its effects and the spacial anomaly and all, but… eek

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thing to do with nostalgia, coupled with the fact they're made of felt, is what just about swung me from an downvote to an upvote. I'm still not sure if it's interesting enough, but there's an odd sense of despair that I like about the piece. That said, I don't think the first addendum adds anything to the article.

Also, gosh that centipede is cute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't intrested in it..

But i still like it somehow +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm already somewhat nostalgia-averse, so these bother me in interesting ways…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How would you go about surgically removing something that mobilizes around the body via minor space anomaly?

EDIT: Sorry not trying to be snarky, this was brought up earlier while this was being drafted. If you have a way and think it'd work here I wouldn't mind putting it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hrm.

While I absolutely love the interview log, I confess to not quite "getting" it. Also, I may have missed it, is it possible to surgically remove them? Has this been attempted?

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH GOD AUUUUGGGHH.

I have no idea what it is about this that skeeved me out. Likely my good ability to imagine what something would feel like, and so this, just, I.

AUUUUGGGHHHH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Normally parasite SCPs don't do anything for me, but somehow every element if this pushes my buttons. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new addendum does certainly add something, but I'm still very meh on this as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Things what turn your body into a bed and breakfast then. My general meaning/point is the same.

(also austin woot woot)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's fair if you're not interested, but this isn't a parasite. It's just something that moves around in your body.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't find parasites at all interesting in general, and this isn't an exception.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: FEEX

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooooone more thing:

They are to be denied access to any toys, music, books and other recreational items manufactured or developed within 12 calendar months

The way I understand this sentence, they are only allowed things that are more then 1 year old. Did you mean the opposite: only recent toys, so they don't get nostalgia?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like going into their breeding cycles outside of the article would honestly fill it with stuff unrelated to what I was trying to get at and left it out.

What does that mean exactly?

eg. SCP-XXXX-17, SCP-XXXX-18, SCP-XXXX-19

Typically adults range between… and are…

Feex

should be hypothesized

Feex

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus fucking Christ. Having a felt centipede under your skin is bad. Making it kill your inability to reminisce pleasantly is worse.

Take this upvote and make my organs not feel like sweaty sheets ;A;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really love the concept. No later then last evening, a fairly large house centipede actually crawled out of my sink.

+1, but I think it could be even better and I encourage you to work on it some more. For instance, why don't people just go and get it checked by a doctor? If it's impossible to remove, that should be better explained.

What about their breeding? Does it happen when they are outside the body?

Some comments on the text itself:

Tubs are to be labeled in accordance with each instance.

What does that mean exactly?

A typical adult SCP-1353 individual can range … and are…

should be: Adults typically range between… and are…

it is theorized

should be hypothesized

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt we needed more centipede SCP's so I wrote one. I've been fascinated by the idea of watching a centipede crawl just beneath your skin for a while now and even considered getting a tattoo of such. I figure I'd put the idea to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frankly, I don't think this SCP has a leg to stand on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First thought upon seeing the picture: "Awwww, adorable lintipede!"

Second thought: "This is going to be horrifying."

Then I read it, and I was not disappointed.
